Wyandra Queensland: Free Camping on the Warrego River
Stop at Wyandra halfway between Cunnamulla and Charleville. Free camping, outback burgers at the post office, a river beach and some of outback Queensland's best sunsets.

name: Boulia
title: Boulia: A week chasing the Min Min
excerpt: Three days out to Boulia via Poddy Creek and the Middleton Hotel, the Min Min Encounter, outback sunsets and no actual Min Min sightings. Then north to a spectacular free camp at Dajarra Dam on the way to Mount Isa.

name: Longreach
title: Things to Do in Longreach: Our Favourites
excerpt: After three visits, here's what we'd actually send a friend to do in Longreach! Qantas history, outback rail and river tours, and a stockman's show. Not everything, just our favourites.

name: Longreach
title: Longreach: Rail, River and a Town That Waves Back
excerpt: Back in Longreach for our third visit. Outback rail adventures, a sunset cruise on the Thomson River, the Longreach Races, new Akubras, and the friendliest welcome yet.

name: Blackall
title: Blackall: Wool, Steam and Unbroken Records
excerpt: Blackall's Historical Woolscour is the only surviving steam-powered woolscour in Australia, operating since 1908. Add Jackie Howe's unbroken shearing record and a free camp on the Barcoo River.

name: Tambo
title: Tambo: Teddies, Chickens and Qantas History
excerpt: Tambo delivers more than it advertises — Tambo Teddies and a station side quest, chicken races that got out of hand, and the site of Qantas's first fatal crash in 1927. A small town with a lot going on.

name: Charleville
title: Charleville: Finding the Hidden Gems
excerpt: Four visits to Charleville and still finding new things — the Angellala Creek explosion site, the Airfield Museum, the Bureau of Meteorology weather balloon and the WWII quarry that built the airport runways.

name: Charleville
title: Things to Do in Charleville: Slow Down, Stay Longer
excerpt: From the Cosmos Centre and Bilby Experience to the Airfield Museum, WWII Secret Base, Outback Date Farm, and the Angellala Creek explosion site. There's a lot going on!

name: Wyandra
title: Wyandra: Sunsets and Silence
excerpt: Halfway between Cunnamulla and Charleville, Wyandra is a quiet railway town on the Warrego River with a sandy beach, outback burgers, a peaceful camp and sunsets good enough for a calendar cover.

name: Cunnamulla
title: Cunnamulla: Rails, Races and Hot Springs
excerpt: An overnight pub camp at Enngonia, then north to Cunnamulla; the Robbers Tree, the All Aboard rail show, a day at the races for Neil and artesian hot springs for Cameron. A weekend well spent.

name: Bourke
title: Bourke: Where the Darling Tells the Story
excerpt: Five rain-soaked days at Nyngan Weir and then north to Bourke, the Darling River's great port, Fred Hollows' resting place, an Afghan mosque in the outback, and a town that rewards anyone who slows down.

name: Hay
title: Hay: History on the Murrumbidgee
excerpt: From Melbourne via Warrnambool and St Arnaud to Hay, a town that keeps earning return visits. Free camping on the Murrumbidgee, a gaol with many lives, and one of regional NSW's most joyful festivals.

name: Warrnambool
title: A Slow Day in Warrnambool
excerpt: A slow morning in the mist at Warrnambool's historic Botanic Gardens, a drive out to Hopkins Falls running at full strength, and an afternoon browsing Fletcher Jones Market.

name: Winchelsea
title: Winchelsea: Bluestone, Rabbits, and the Barwon
excerpt: Two nights on the Barwon River at Winchelsea's free camp, and a guided tour of Barwon Park Mansion, the bluestone homestead built to impress a duke, by the man who gave Australia its rabbit problem.

name: Ararat
title: Ararat: Visiting J Ward
excerpt: J Ward in Ararat began as a gold rush gaol before becoming Victoria's Criminally Insane Division. A guided tour reveals bluestone cells, preserved gallows, and a confronting chapter in Australia's justice and mental health history.

name: Ararat
title: Ararat: Inside Aradale
excerpt: Aradale in Ararat is one of Australia's largest former psychiatric hospitals. A guided history tour reveals confronting stories, evolving mental health care, and the complex legacy of an institution that operated for more than a century.

name: Queenscliff
title: Queenscliff: Dining on The Q Train
excerpt: A moving degustation through Bellarine farmland, The Q Train pairs heritage rail with seriously good food. From steam departures to thoughtful non-alcoholic pairings, it's one of Victoria's most memorable regional dining experiences.

name: Werribee
title: Werribee Open Range Zoo: A Safari Near the City
excerpt: Werribee Open Range Zoo offers a surprisingly immersive safari experience just outside Melbourne. With open savannahs, accessible paths, and thoughtful design, it feels far removed from the city while remaining easy to navigate.
